description_long: This object is a straight-edged sword with a double-edged blade, featuring a metallic sheen and a pointed tip. The blade is long and tapers gradually to a sharp point, suitable for thrusting and slashing. The handle is ornate, with a dark, possibly wooden or metal grip, and a prominent pommel and crossguard, both of which are decorated with gold or brass accents. The sword comes with a matching sheath that is dark in color, likely made of leather or metal, and features metallic fittings and a decorative band. The overall design suggests a historical or ceremonial European sword, possibly a Roman gladius or similar short sword. The craftsmanship appears high-quality, with attention to both function and decorative detail.
description: A straight, double-edged sword with a decorated handle and matching dark sheath, featuring gold or brass accents and a pointed blade.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the sword and sheath, highlighting the blade's straight edge and the ornate handle and fittings.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thinness of the blade and sheath, with minimal detail visible except the profile of the handle and fittings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the sword and sheath, with visible decorative bands and fittings.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, showing the handle's profile and the rounded pommel and sheath fittings.
attribute_handle_design: straight, dark grip with gold/brass pommel and crossguard, decorative accents
attribute_ornamentation: decorative gold/brass accents on handle, guard, and sheath
